description_long: This object is a robust, rectangular bollard constructed from what appears to be solid concrete or stone, featuring a tall, vertical column with a slightly wider square base and cap. The surface is textured with visible weathering, stains, and subtle discoloration, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The column is inset with shallow rectangular panels on each face, adding structural detail and visual interest. The cap is flat and extends slightly beyond the width of the column, providing a defined top edge. The base is also square and slightly flared, giving the bollard stability and a grounded appearance. There are no visible openings, connectors, or decorative elements beyond the geometric paneling.
description: A tall, rectangular concrete or stone bollard with a flat cap and base, featuring inset panel details and a weathered, textured surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular column with inset paneling and a flat, overhanging cap and base.
description_view_1: Side view reveals similar paneling and the same flat cap and base, with subtle surface stains.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the same panel details and weathered texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the rectangular panel and the consistent flat cap and base.
